title: Untitled document.pdf
summaries:
* The Himalayas is a mountain range in Asia separating the plains of the Indian subcontinent from the Tibetan Plateau.
* The range includes Mount Everest and more than 100 peaks exceeding elevations of 7,200 metres (23,600 feet) above sea level.
* It spans five countries: Nepal, India, China, Bhutan, and Pakistan, with sovereignty in the Kashmir region disputed among India, Pakistan, and China.
* The range is bordered on the northwest by the Karakoram and Hindu Kush ranges, on the north by the Tibetan Plateau, and on the south by the Indo-Gangetic Plain.
* Major rivers including the Indus, the Ganges, and the Tsangpo–Brahmaputra rise in the vicinity of the Himalayas, with a combined drainage basin home to 600 million people.
* 53 million people live in the Himalayas.

* The combined drainage basin of the Himalayas is home to some 600 million people, and 53 million people live in the mountains.
* Many Himalayan peaks are sacred in Hinduism and Buddhism, shaping the cultures of South Asia and Tibet.
* Several summits are off-limits to climbers, including Kangchenjunga (from the Indian side), Gangkhar Puensum, Machapuchare, Nanda Devi, and Kailash in the Tibetan Transhimalaya.
* The Himalayas were uplifted by the collision of the Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates, specifically by the folding, or nappe-formation, of the uppermost Indian crust.
* The mountain range runs west-northwest to east-southeast in an arc 2,400 km (1,500 mi) long.
* Its western anchor is Nanga Parba.

* An unspecified entity, along with the mantle, subducted under Eurasia.
* The Himalayan mountain range runs west-northwest to east-southeast in an arc 2,400 km (1,500 mi) long.
* The range's western anchor is Nanga Parbat, and its eastern anchor is Namcha Barwa.
* The Indus-Yarlung suture zone separates the Himalayas from the Tibetan plateau.
* The Indus and Yarlung Tsangpo rivers separate the Himalayas from the Karakorams, the Hindu Kush, and the Transhimalaya.
* The range varies in width from 350 km (220 mi) in the west to 151 km (94 mi) in the east.
